core_mechanism: a bistable stochastic process with weak mean-reverting feedback and long (non-exponential) memory, producing two metastable states with long residence times and slowly-decaying autocorrelation instead of fast exponential relaxation to a single equilibrium
brief_reason: The abstract describes a slowly-evolving latent variable whose corrective (mean-reverting) feedback weakens, yielding increasing bimodality, longer residence times in optimistic/pessimistic regimes, and shocks that leave longer traces than short-memory exponential decay would predict.
